dimensions.D1: Technical correctness (0/0.5/1.0) - factual accuracy of architecture claims against ground truth
dimensions.D2: Citation of prior art (0.0-1.0 ratio) - named references to VW-NNN, ADR-NNN, file paths, document titles
dimensions.D3: Completeness (0.0-1.0) - coverage of ground truth sections and claims
dimensions.D4: Conciseness (0/0.5/1.0) - signal-to-noise, no filler or hallucinated padding
dimensions.D5: Pattern adoption (0.0-1.0) - follows ADR structure, frontmatter, Rootweaver conventions

rationale.D1: 1.0 - Technically precise against GT and implementation-verified. (1) Correctly identifies Kafka topic vault.connector-docs; (2) ConnectorDoc model fields match GT exactly (source, source_id, title, content, url, author, created_at, updated_at, metadata); (3) Single Qdrant collection rootweaver_vault with source facet; (4) KEDA ScaledJob deployment model; (5) Correctly names the Onyx shim approach with OnyxBridge and CheckpointedConnector; (6) 9 connector sources listed with accurate line counts (1,394 Jira, 2,883 Confluence, etc.); (7) UUID5-based deterministic point IDs for upsert idempotency; (8) 750-word chunks with 75-word overlap; (9) Watermark-based incremental sync with Postgres state; (10) Correctly identifies VW-186 source filter bug and commit c392e56 fix; (11) Agent path source filter gap honestly disclosed. No factual contradictions with GT found. The 4-layer architecture (Onyx Shim, Connector Sources, Bridge/Models, Factory/Indexer) is a valid decomposition of GT's design.
rationale.D2: 0.9 - Highest citation density among the three variants. Named references: VW-184 (correct parent issue), VW-186 (with commit c392e56), VW-188 (231 issues synced), VW-232 (indexer __main__ blocker), VW-243 (DLQ replay design), VW-235 (dual-write), ADR-013 (SealedSecrets -- GT explicitly references this), ADR-016 (Modular Architecture Documentation). File paths: packages/rootweaver-connectors/ with bridge.py, models.py, factory.py, indexer.py, onyx_shim/, sources/. Vault plan paths: Connector Framework Design plan, VW-184 implementation report, DLQ Replay Design. External: Onyx github URL. Missing from GT: vault_indexer.py path (neural-vault/vault_indexer.py), mcp_bridge.py path (rag_platform/bridges/mcp_bridge.py), federation.py path, Kafka topic names vault.file-events/vault.file-tagged. Adds references not in GT (VW-235, VW-243 plan path). Ratio: ~10/12 GT references present + ~6 additional verified references.
rationale.D3: 0.85 - Covers all major GT sections. Context: single-source limitation, current state (paragraph form, not table), market comparison (via 'Evidence from Retrieval Tools' section that also probes memory/vault/code layers), Onyx analysis. Decision: 4-layer architecture, ConnectorDoc model, 9 connector table with lines and content indexed, Qdrant single-collection strategy with rationale, scheduling and state management, source filter plumbing. Consequences: 7 positive, 5 negative, 6 risks with mitigations table. Alternatives: 4 (adds Airbyte over GT's 3). Implementation Status: table with component/status/evidence columns. References: 10+ named refs. Missing from GT: (1) explicit 'Why Kafka Not Direct Writes' numbered list (5 reasons -- content is present in Qdrant strategy rationale but not as a standalone section); (2) 'Comparison: Current MCP vs Proposed Connectors' table (GT's 7-row comparison); (3) P0/P1/P2/P3 priority and effort table; (4) explicit scheduling interval table; (5) BaseConnector ABC code block with poll_source/incremental_sync/validate_credentials signatures; (6) SourceType enum code. The Evidence from Retrieval Tools section is novel and not in GT -- adds value as it shows the condition's retrieval methodology.
rationale.D4: 1.0 - Every paragraph is load-bearing. The 4-layer architecture decomposition is precise and scannable. Technical claims include specific counts (463 lines framework, 9,209 lines sources, 231 Jira issues, ~1,400 lines shim). Source filter plumbing honestly documents partial completion (gateway fixed, agent path deferred). Implementation Status table concisely shows what's done vs blocked vs not started. The 'Evidence from Retrieval Tools' section could be considered padding in a pure ADR context, but it serves the Condition E prompt's requirement to show retrieval methodology. No filler sentences, no vague generalizations, no hallucinated details detected.
rationale.D5: 0.95 - Full ADR structure: Status, Context, Decision (with 6 subsections), Consequences (positive/negative/risks with mitigations), Implementation Status, Alternatives Considered (4), References. Frontmatter has title, status, date, type, jira (VW-184 correct), vault-path (09-System/Architecture/ADRs -- matches GT), tags. Missing: (1) GT's Deciders field; (2) GT's granular project/*/tech/* tags (has generic adr/architecture/connectors/ingestion/rag only). The 'Evidence from Retrieval Tools' section in Context is non-standard for an ADR but appropriate for Condition E's retrieval-augmented methodology. Related references section well-formatted with both vault paths and issue references.

rationale.D1: 1.0 - Technically precise and implementation-verified with deep architectural reasoning. (1) Correctly identifies vault.connector-docs Kafka topic; (2) ConnectorDoc model matches GT fields; (3) Single Qdrant collection rootweaver_vault; (4) OnyxBridge checkpoint_sync correctly described with (produced, failures, max_updated_at) return tuple; (5) 9 connectors listed with correct credential env vars; (6) Watermark-based incremental sync with Postgres state (2000-01-01 initial watermark); (7) UUID5 deterministic point IDs; (8) 750-word chunks, 75-word overlap; (9) K3s CronJob scheduling with detailed rationale vs Prefect (Path A vs B decision); (10) QdrantAdapter dual-write constraint (VW-235) with inline code comment; (11) Source filter fix commit c392e56 and three repair sites in build_filter(); (12) Agent path gap (VW-186-part-2) honestly disclosed; (13) 231 Jira issues end-to-end verified with Postgres watermark value. No factual contradictions. The Path B (CronJob vs Prefect) analysis adds verifiable decision rationale not in GT but consistent with the architecture.
rationale.D2: 0.85 - Strong citation density with implementation-level specificity. Named references: VW-184, VW-186 (with commit c392e56 and 3 call-site repairs), VW-188 (231 issues, Postgres watermark value), VW-232 (missing __main__ block), VW-235 (QdrantAdapter dual-write), VW-185 (HashiCorp Vault deferred). File paths: bridge.py, models.py, factory.py, indexer.py, indexer_runner.py, onyx_shim/onyx/connectors/. Onyx type names: BaseConnector, CheckpointedConnector, ConnectorCheckpoint, Document, ConnectorFailure, HierarchyNode. Onyx github URL. Session reference: 5f5aba56. SealedSecrets pattern mentioned (ADR-013 by concept, not by number). Missing from GT: (1) ADR-013 not cited by number; (2) ADR-016 not cited; (3) vault_indexer.py path; (4) mcp_bridge.py path; (5) federation.py path; (6) vault.file-events/vault.file-tagged topic names. Adds references beyond GT: VW-235, Prefect session 5f5aba56. Ratio: ~7/12 GT references + ~5 additional.
rationale.D3: 0.75 - Covers core GT sections but with different structural emphasis. Context: single-source limitation with 3 capabilities blocked, market comparison (brief mention of Glean 100+ and Onyx 40+), VW-186 source filter bug as concrete evidence. Decision: 6 numbered architectural decisions (Onyx shim, Kafka boundary, watermark sync, CronJob scheduling, ConnectorIndexer consumer, source filter plumbing) -- a different decomposition than GT's but covering similar content. 'Rationale for key choices' standalone section. Consequences: positive (5), negative/open risks (6 combined). Alternatives: 3 (custom clients, Prefect Path A, separate collections -- different set from GT's Onyx wholesale/MCP-only/MCP tools, though content overlaps). Status and next steps. Missing from GT: (1) current state table (GT's 5-source table); (2) explicit market comparison table; (3) detailed Onyx analysis (7 points -- only brief mention); (4) 'Why Kafka Not Direct Writes' numbered section; (5) 'Why Not Adopt Onyx Wholesale' numbered section; (6) BaseConnector ABC code block; (7) SourceType enum code; (8) P0/P1/P2/P3 priority table; (9) 'Current MCP vs Proposed Connectors' comparison table; (10) scheduling interval table; (11) Qdrant Option A vs B analysis; (12) 3-phase implementation plan. The 6-decision structure provides deep rationale but sacrifices GT's breadth.
rationale.D4: 1.0 - Exceptionally dense and precise. Every section carries implementation-verified detail. The 6-decision structure is tightly organized with specific code snippets (ConnectorDoc dataclass, VW-235 comment), verified metrics (231 issues, Postgres watermark timestamp), and honest status disclosure (consumer blocked VW-232, agent filter not wired VW-186-part-2). The 'Rationale for key choices' section adds genuine architectural reasoning (why Kafka as boundary, why credential-gated activation) that enriches the ADR. HierarchyNode dropped intentionally -- a design choice documented rather than hidden. ConnectorFailure handling documented (log and continue). No filler, no hand-waving, no hallucinated details.
rationale.D5: 0.8 - Has ADR structure but deviates from GT's standard ADR template. Frontmatter has title, date, type, status, jira (VW-188 -- should be VW-184 per GT), tags. Missing from frontmatter: (1) vault-path (GT has this); (2) Deciders field; (3) jira references VW-188 as primary but GT uses VW-184. Document structure: Context, Decision (6 numbered decisions), Rationale for key choices, Consequences, Alternatives considered, Status and next steps. Deviations: (1) 'Rationale for key choices' as standalone section is non-standard (GT folds rationale into Decision); (2) 'Status and next steps' replaces GT's 'Implementation Plan' with its phased format; (3) No 'References' section (GT has explicit References list); (4) Uses --- horizontal rules extensively. The numbered decision structure is clear but not the GT's pattern. Tags are minimal (5 vs GT's 18+).

rationale.D1: 0.5 - Mostly correct but with several factual errors and inconsistencies. Correct: (1) Kafka topic vault.connector-docs; (2) Single Qdrant collection rootweaver_vault; (3) KEDA ScaledJob pattern; (4) Onyx BaseConnector pattern; (5) VW-184 parent issue; (6) VW-186 source filter fix commit c392e56; (7) VW-188 231 issues synced; (8) VW-232 consumer __main__ gap. Incorrect or inconsistent: (1) Architecture diagram says 'Chunk (512-token, 0.1 overlap)' but GT says Qwen3-Embedding-4B/2560d embedding model -- Haiku instead uses Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B/1024d which matches the older vault indexer config, not the GT's connector indexer spec; (2) Evidence Layer 1 table lists 'State Manager | state.py | Redis-backed incremental sync state' but the Sonnet and Opus variants (and GT) describe Postgres-backed watermark state, not Redis; (3) Evidence Layer 1 lists 'Scheduler | scheduler.py | Prefect flows: 15-min incremental, weekly full re-sync' but Sonnet's analysis shows K3s CronJob was selected over Prefect (Path B decision); (4) ConnectorDoc code uses 'metadata: Dict[str, Any] = None' instead of GT's 'metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)' -- using None as default for a mutable type is a Python anti-pattern; (5) Evidence Layer 2 claims 'Cross-source benchmarking blocked without this fix' as a 'Fact' from auto_search_vault but this reads as fabricated retrieval output; (6) References section cites 'Temporal Filtering Analysis: Vault search improvements +40 percentage points (VW-107)' -- VW-107 in GT/MEMORY is about Apple MPS memory management, not temporal filtering. Several details appear to be confidently stated but contradict the ground truth or other verified variants.
rationale.D2: 0.75 - Good citation density but includes at least one incorrect reference. Named: VW-184 (correct), VW-186 (with commit c392e56), VW-188, VW-232, ADR-013 (SealedSecrets -- GT names this), ADR-016, ADR-018 (Kafka + KEDA). File paths: rag_platform/connectors/ (older path, not packages/rootweaver-connectors/), rag_platform/core/vector_stores/qdrant.py, tests/unit/test_qdrant_source_filter.py, rootweaver-gitops paths. Onyx github URL. External links to vault HLD/LLD/benchmark docs. Missing from GT: vault_indexer.py path (neural-vault/vault_indexer.py), mcp_bridge.py path, federation.py path, vault.file-events/vault.file-tagged topic names. Incorrect: VW-107 cited as 'Temporal Filtering Analysis +40 percentage points' but VW-107 is actually Apple MPS memory management. Uses old code path (rag_platform/connectors/) rather than post-ADR-030 packages/rootweaver-connectors/. Ratio: ~8/12 GT references present, but quality reduced by incorrect VW-107 attribution.
rationale.D3: 0.9 - Broadest structural coverage of the three variants, closely mirroring GT's section layout. Has: (1) Current state table (matches GT's 4-source table minus Web row); (2) Market comparison table; (3) Onyx architecture reference (6 points vs GT's 7); (4) ASCII architecture diagram (closely matches GT's layout); (5) 'Why Kafka' section (5 numbered reasons matching GT); (6) 'Why Not Onyx Wholesale' (5 reasons matching GT); (7) BaseConnector ABC code with poll_source/incremental_sync/validate_credentials (matches GT); (8) Priority table with P0/P1/P2/P3 (adds Status column beyond GT); (9) Qdrant collection strategy; (10) Scheduling interval table (matching GT's intervals); (11) 3 Evidence Layers (novel, not in GT); (12) Consequences with positive/negative/risks; (13) 3 alternatives matching GT; (14) 3-phase implementation plan (matching GT's structure); (15) References section. Missing: (1) 'Comparison: Current MCP vs Proposed Connectors' table (GT's 7-dimension table); (2) SourceType enum code block; (3) Deployment model details (GT's KEDA ScaledJob + SealedSecrets paragraph). The Evidence Layer sections are additive but inflate length substantially.
rationale.D4: 0.5 - Mixed signal-to-noise. The document is the longest of the three variants at ~445 lines. Several sections add genuine value (Evidence Layer 1 with VW-188 verification data, Evidence Layer 2 decision recall, Evidence Layer 3 architecture search confirmation). However: (1) Evidence Layer 2 (6 lines, 3 'Facts') reads as fabricated recall output with generic assertions rather than verified data; (2) Evidence Layer 3 (9 lines) restates ADR references already covered in the main text; (3) The architecture diagram is essentially copy-pasted from GT with only chunking parameters changed -- this is high fidelity but not additive analysis; (4) The BaseConnector code block is nearly identical to GT's code; (5) The 'Why Kafka' and 'Why Not Onyx' sections closely mirror GT's wording; (6) Related Documentation section at the end references vault HLD/LLD pages that may not exist (speculative paths). Several sections duplicate GT rather than synthesize from retrieval, reducing the ratio of novel insight to reproduced content.
rationale.D5: 0.85 - Good ADR structural compliance. Frontmatter has title, date, type, status, jira (VW-184 correct, plus VW-186/VW-188/VW-232 -- GT only has VW-184), deciders, vault-path (09-System/Architecture/ADRs). Has executive summary (not in GT pattern but a useful addition). Standard ADR sections: Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives, Implementation Status, References. Uses emoji status indicators in tables (not in GT pattern but common in project docs). Has horizontal rule separators (---) between major sections. Missing: GT's granular project/*/tech/* tags (has only 5 generic tags). The multiple Jira references in frontmatter jira field is non-standard (GT uses single issue). Related Documentation section at the end adds vault cross-references. Overall closely follows GT's ADR template with minor deviations.

opus.strengths: Implementation-verified 4-layer architecture with precise line counts and component analysis, highest citation density (VW-184/186/188/232/243/235 + ADR-013/016), honest disclosure of partial completion (source filter agent gap, VW-232 consumer blocker), Evidence from Retrieval Tools section shows retrieval methodology transparently
opus.weaknesses: Missing GT's BaseConnector ABC code block and SourceType enum, missing P0/P1/P2/P3 priority table, missing scheduling interval table, missing 'Current MCP vs Proposed Connectors' comparison table

sonnet.strengths: Deepest architectural reasoning (6 numbered decisions with explicit rationale), CronJob vs Prefect Path A/B analysis not in GT but implementation-verified, QdrantAdapter dual-write constraint documented with code comment, exceptionally dense writing with zero filler
sonnet.weaknesses: Frontmatter jira references VW-188 not VW-184, missing vault-path in frontmatter, missing current state table and market comparison table, missing BaseConnector code and SourceType enum, narrower structural coverage than GT (no priority table, no scheduling table, no implementation phases)

haiku.strengths: Broadest structural coverage mirroring GT's section layout (has priority table, scheduling table, code blocks, architecture diagram, 'Why Kafka', 'Why Not Onyx' numbered lists), 3 Evidence Layers show retrieval methodology, good ADR frontmatter compliance with vault-path
haiku.weaknesses: Factual errors: Redis-backed state (should be Postgres), Prefect scheduler (should be CronJob per Path B decision), VW-107 misattributed as temporal filtering (actually MPS memory), ConnectorDoc metadata default None anti-pattern, uses old rag_platform/ paths instead of post-ADR-030 packages/, several sections closely reproduce GT text rather than synthesize from retrieval, longest variant with lower signal-to-noise

haiku_e_vs_opus_a.analysis: Haiku+E (0.70) beats Opus+A (0.51) by +0.19. The retrieval augmentation in Condition E gives Haiku access to verified implementation details (VW-188 231 issues, VW-186 commit c392e56, VW-232 consumer gap) that Opus+A lacked entirely, raising its citation score from what would be near-zero to 0.75. Haiku+E also achieves 0.9 completeness by reproducing GT's structural sections (priority table, scheduling table, code blocks) -- sections that Opus+A partially hallucinated with wrong topic names and VW-TBD. However, Haiku+E's technical correctness (0.5) is dragged down by factual errors (Redis vs Postgres, Prefect vs CronJob, VW-107 misattribution) that Opus+A did not make in the same domains. The net result: retrieval augmentation compensates for a weaker base model on citation and completeness, but the weaker model still makes synthesis errors when combining retrieved facts.
